name: TensorWave
description: AMD-focused GPU cloud specializing in MI300X and MI325X accelerators for AI training and inference.

seo.title: TensorWave — Backend-as-a-Service
seo.meta_description: TensorWave: AMD-focused GPU cloud specializing in MI300X and MI325X accelerators for AI training and inference.

when_to_use: AMD MI300X GPU clusters for AI training at competitive pricing
LLM training on AMD accelerators as H100 alternative
High-bandwidth memory GPUs for large-model training runs
Cost-efficient AI training without NVIDIA vendor dependency
Burst GPU capacity on AMD hardware for ML research teams

pros: Cheaper per-GPU than Nvidia H100 alternatives
Higher HBM capacity suits large-context inference
cons: ROCm tooling still lags CUDA maturity
Smaller software ecosystem vs Nvidia GPUs
Some kernels need manual porting from CUDA
Younger company with less track record
review.verdict: A credible AMD-first GPU cloud if your stack tolerates ROCm, cheaper HBM that Nvidia shops will struggle to use.
review.best_for: Inference teams chasing HBM-heavy workloads and willing to work in ROCm
review.not_for: CUDA-only shops or teams needing mature Nvidia tooling and ecosystem

pricing.high_water_mark_reason: Usage-based metered billing scales up and down

lock_in.reason: AMD GPU cloud, standard VM access
